summary: Soaring Dark Star and transcendent second‑set jams
blurb: The show shines with a monumental Dark Star that launches a soaring second set, highlighted by Attics Of My Life and an epic Death Don't Have No Mercy → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude run. Jerry’s vocals and Brent’s keyboards are in top form, making this a must‑listen late‑’80s performance.
review: October 9, 1989 at the Hampton Coliseum delivered one of the Grateful Dead’s most electrifying late‑period nights. The first set rolls along with solid staples like Feel Like A Stranger and Built To Last, but it’s the second set that truly lifts the show – a blistering Playing In The Band → Uncle John’s Band → Playing In The Band tease erupts into a legendary Dark Star that bursts with Jerry’s soaring tone (even his new MIDI‑synth ‘Wolf’ guitar gets rave mentions). From there the band rides a powerful groove through Drums, Space and an unforgettable Death Don't Have No Mercy → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale, before closing with a heartfelt Attics Of My Life encore. Brent’s keyboards and vocals sparkle throughout, while Phil’s bass anchors the improvisations and both Mickey and Billy drive the rhythmic flow with precision. Across dozens of recordings fans consistently describe this night as “legendary,” “epic,” and “one of the best Dark Star outings of the era.”

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Overwhelmingly positive sentiment across all recordings: multiple reviewers call the Dark Star "soaring" and "legendary," praise the second‑set flow, highlight Brent’s keyboard/vocal work, and note Jerry’s vocal/solo brilliance. Only a few minor criticisms (flubbed Row Jimmy, wrecked Uncle John’s) appear, but they do not outweigh the consistent acclaim.

best_recording.reason: Miller’s pristine soundboard mix is praised as "better than the boxed set," delivering crystal‑clear detail and capturing the legendary second‑set without technical flaws.

key_highlights: Monumental Dark Star that launches a soaring second set
Transcendent Death Don't Have No Mercy → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude sequence
Emotional Attics Of My Life encore
Powerful Playing In The Band jam that feeds into the Dark Star

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring vocals and a searing Dark Star solo, with his new MIDI‑synth ‘Wolf’ tone drawing particular praise.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid, booming bass foundation that underpins the improvisations; his presence is noted as especially powerful in audience recordings.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds tight rhythm guitar work and complementary vocal harmonies throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards shine on Dark Star and Attics Of My Life, while his vocals are highlighted as outstanding, adding fresh textures to the late‑period sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart drives the rhythmic flow with precise percussion, contributing to the dynamic second‑set energy.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming locks in tightly with Hart, delivering powerful grooves that propel the jams.
